artifactCache.title: Artifact Cache
artifactCache.drawerDesc: Node-local core jars cached by sha256; instance creation copies on hit, no re-download. Global artifact library is managed in the control plane.
artifactCache.empty: No cached items yet. Core jars are cached automatically when creating instances.
artifactCache.evictDesc: This removes the local cache for "{{name}}"; the next instance creation re-downloads it.
artifactCache.clearDesc: Removes all cached core jars on this node; future instance creation re-downloads. Running instances are unaffected.
artifactCache.jdkDrawerDesc: Manage this node's JDKs: download (multi-vendor / specific version) or register an existing one (directory picker).
artifactCache.jdkInstallDispatched: JDK install task dispatched; track progress in Task Center
artifactCache.jdkInstallHint: Downloads go through the outbound proxy; specific versions are resolved via foojay. Track progress in Task Center.

danger.denied: Your role is not allowed to perform this action. Contact an administrator.
danger.deleteInstanceDesc: This action cannot be undone. All instance data will be deleted.
danger.killInstanceDesc: Force-stop immediately terminates the process and may lose unsaved data. Use only when the instance is stuck starting or stopping.
danger.deleteNodeDesc: The node will be unregistered; its records are kept. Reconnecting requires re-registration.
danger.deleteUserDesc: This action cannot be undone. The user will no longer be able to sign in.
danger.deleteGroupDesc: This action cannot be undone. Group memberships and quota will be removed.
danger.batchKillDesc: Selected instance processes will be killed; unsaved data may be lost.

probe.updatedPending: Probe pushed; takes effect on next restart

business.title: Business Console

business.reasonPlaceholder: Record why you run this; written to plugin ledger and platform audit

economy.title: Economy Console
economy.subtitle: Balance / Leaderboard / Transfer / Ledger (from aggregated mirror; writes dispatched to real mce via probe)
economy.unavailable: No economy capability on this server or probe not connected; install the economy plugin (MultiCurrencyEconomy) and connect the probe
economy.amountPlaceholder: Positive number, sent as-is string (no float)
economy.amountInvalid: Amount must be a number greater than 0
economy.balanceHint: Query aggregated balance by player (+ optional currency), one row per node/zone (cross-zone not merged)
economy.leaderboardHint: Top-N by balance for a currency (bypass: derived from platform mirror, not via probe)
economy.leaderboardNeedCurrency: Leaderboard requires a currency (cross-currency balances are not comparable)
economy.transferHint: Initiate a real transfer / adjust; writes require confirmation; amount sent as string without precision loss
economy.ledgerHint: Query economy change ledger by player (from aggregated events, each entry recorded)
economy.reasonPlaceholder: Record why you run this; written to plugin ledger and platform audit
economy.confirmTransferDesc: About to transfer {{amount}} {{currency}} from {{from}} to {{to}} (real economy data). A mistake may cause loss of funds — confirm before proceeding.
economy.confirmAdjustDesc: About to {{action}} {{amount}} {{currency}} for {{player}} (real economy data). A mistake may dupe or mis-deduct currency — confirm before proceeding.

inventory.title: Inventory Console
inventory.subtitle: Player inventory snapshot / item list (via probe inventory Provider; writes use nbtBase64 as source of truth)

inventory.viewHint: Enter a player UUID to view their inventory snapshot (loaded from source incl. offline; read-only, does not affect the real inventory)
inventory.playerPlaceholder: Player UUID (loaded from source, incl. offline players)
inventory.notFound: Player {{player}} has no persisted inventory data (unlike an empty inventory: this player was never recorded)

serverState.unavailable: State collection did not succeed this time

nodes.jdkInUse: JDK is in use by instances: {{names}}

enroll.addDesc: Issue a one-time install credential; paste the one-line command on the target machine to auto-register and come online.
enroll.nodeNamePlaceholder: Leave blank to auto-report from Worker
enroll.resultDesc: Copy and save it now. The credential is one-time and time-limited; the full command cannot be shown again after closing.
enroll.hint: If no public download source is configured, copy the Worker binary to the target machine first and append --binary <path> (use -Binary <path> on Windows).
enroll.manualTitle: Manual install steps (step-by-step fallback / inspect first)
enroll.copyFailed: Copy failed, please select and copy manually
enroll.tabAutoDesc: Run the one-line script on the target machine to auto-download, install the Worker, and register online.
enroll.tabManualDesc: If the Worker binary is already deployed, fill in the address and one-time token below and start it to register — no install script needed.
enroll.manualConnectStep1: 1. Control Plane address (set as control-plane in the Worker config)
enroll.manualConnectStep2: 2. One-time enroll token (set as enroll-token in the Worker config)
enroll.manualConnectStep3: 3. Start the Worker on the target machine (with arguments)
enroll.manualConnectHint: The token is one-time and time-limited; it is invalidated once registration succeeds. The first registration must complete before expiry.

nodes.selectHint: Select a node on the left to view details

nodeProxy.title: Outbound proxy
nodeProxy.desc: This node's downloads (JDK / server jar / self-update) go through this proxy. Changes are pushed via heartbeat and take effect at runtime — no need to edit worker.yaml or restart.
nodeProxy.inheritHint: Use the platform global default proxy
nodeProxy.pendingPush: Node offline; takes effect on the next heartbeat after it comes online
nodeProxy.saved: Saved; will be pushed to the node via heartbeat
nodeProxy.maskedHint: Addresses containing credentials are masked when displayed; re-enter the full address to change it.

nodeRepair.unavailable: Node repair is not enabled on this deployment.
nodeRepair.selfSuspect: This node is flagged as a suspected bad node
nodeRepair.selfHealthy: No identity-conflict signals detected for this node
nodeRepair.reenrollHint: Rotate UUID/secret to cut ties with a hijacked old identity; the old Worker credential is invalidated immediately.
nodeRepair.reenrollConfirmDesc: A brand-new UUID and secret will be issued for this node row. The old credential is invalidated at once; the machine must re-register with the new identity to come back online.
nodeRepair.reenrollDone: Re-enrolled. Save the new secret below.
nodeRepair.reenrollDoneHint: The new secret is shown only once. Copy and apply it to the target machine's Worker config.
nodeRepair.purgeHint: Hard-delete JDK registrations and detach instances wrongly routed to this node during the hijack.
nodeRepair.purgeConfirmDesc: Will delete {{jdk}} JDK registration(s) and detach {{instance}} instance(s) from this node. This is irreversible; running instances are not affected.

clientChannels.title: Client Distribution
clientChannels.subtitle: Manage client distribution channels and pull keys. One channel per server; keys let the player-side updater fetch the manifest and artifacts.
clientChannels.channelIdHint: Lowercase letters/digits/hyphens, 2-64 chars, immutable after creation
clientChannels.deleteChannelDesc: This deletes the channel and all its pull keys. This cannot be undone.
clientChannels.revokeConfirmDesc: Revocation is irreversible: already-distributed clients using this key will no longer be able to update (manifest/artifact pulls are rejected). Only revoke when this key no longer serves any shipped modpack.
clientChannels.keyValuePlaceholder: Leave blank to auto-generate; or enter a custom value
clientChannels.keyValueHint: If customized, it is used as the plaintext; revealable/editable anytime.
clientChannels.editKeyDesc: Change the name or key value. Leave the value blank to rename only; fill it in to change the value.
clientChannels.keyNewValuePlaceholder: Leave blank to keep the value, rename only
clientChannels.editKeyValueWarn: Changing the value invalidates the old value immediately: already-distributed clients holding the old value can no longer update; you must ship the new value to players. Confirm a replacement is truly needed.
clientChannels.secretDesc: Copy and store it now. You will not be able to view the full key again after closing.
clientChannels.copyFailed: Copy failed, please select and copy manually
clientChannels.emptyDesc: A distribution channel is the entry point for player client OTA updates: create a channel, pull a key, publish a version, and integrate the launcher — four steps to deliver updates automatically.
clientChannels.createDialogDesc: Create a distribution channel for one server; after creation you enter the workbench to configure keys and publish versions.
clientChannels.readyAll: All set; players can update normally
clientChannels.stepKeysHint: Create a pull key for the player-side updater to authenticate and fetch.
clientChannels.stepVersionHint: Upload client files and publish the first version so players can fetch latest.
clientChannels.stepIntegrateHint: Follow the integration guide to bundle the updater into your modpack and ship it to players.
clientChannels.keysSubtitle: Pull keys are stored as hashes only; the plaintext is shown once on creation. Save it into jm-updater.json.
clientChannels.createKeyDesc: The plaintext key is shown only once after creation — copy and save it immediately.
clientChannels.createKeyDescViewable: Pull keys are permanent once shipped; you can reveal the plaintext anytime after creation. Leave the value blank to auto-generate.
clientChannels.keysSubtitleViewable: Pull keys are stored with reversible encryption — permanent once shipped, revealable anytime and copyable into jm-updater.json. The key value is editable (changing it invalidates clients holding the old value).
clientChannels.revealDesc: Used by the player-side updater to authenticate pulls. Copy it into jm-updater.json and keep it safe.
clientChannels.notRevealable: This key was created before the reveal feature or without encryption configured; its plaintext cannot be recovered. Use Edit to set it to a known value, then it becomes revealable (note: changing the value invalidates clients holding the old value).

clientVersions.subtitle: Version history is admin-only; players only fetch the latest. Rollback republishes old content under a higher version number and never triggers client downgrade protection.
clientVersions.empty: No versions yet. Click "Publish Version" to start.
clientVersions.rollbackConfirmDesc: This republishes that version's content as the latest under a higher version number (version stays monotonic; clients advance normally and are not rejected by downgrade protection).
clientVersions.wizardDesc: Upload client files (checksums computed automatically), set each file's path and sync policy, then publish. This release publishes uncompressed (codec=none).
clientVersions.managedDirsHint: Comma/newline separated. Only within these dirs are local files not listed in the manifest removed (pruning).
clientVersions.stepFilesDesc: Select the client files to publish (mods, configs, resource packs, etc.). Checksums are computed automatically on upload.
clientVersions.stepConfigureDesc: Set each file's target relative path within the game directory, sync policy, and target platform.
clientVersions.stepReviewDesc: Review the items below, then publish. Publishing switches latest to a higher version number; players will fetch this version.
clientVersions.zipHint: Uploading a .zip unpacks it in the browser and arranges it into the tree below by its internal directory structure; loose files and zips can be mixed and accumulated.
clientVersions.lockedHint: File content is locked (content-addressed sha256 is immutable); you can only arrange path/dir, sync policy, target platform, or remove.
clientVersions.contentLocked: Content locked (content-addressed; bytes cannot be changed, only arranged or removed)
clientVersions.discardDesc: You have an arrangement draft for {{n}} uploaded file(s) (files are on the server, but which to publish and their paths/policies are not). Closing discards this arrangement; you'll need to set it up again.

clientPublish.discardDesc: You have an arrangement draft for {{n}} uploaded file(s) (files are on the server, but which to publish and their paths/policies are not yet published). Leaving discards this arrangement; you'll need to set it up again.

clientCore.subtitle: Centrally manage updater-core versions: upload core jar, pin / update / roll back per channel. The wedge is a fixed single version, not managed here. Roll back by republishing old content under a higher version — never trips client anti-downgrade.
clientCore.noneRegistered: No core version registered yet; manifest keeps the value entered at publish time
clientCore.wedgeFrozen: Version {{v}} · fixed single version, not version-managed (ships with the base pack)
clientCore.rollbackConfirmDesc: Republishes this version's core bytes under a higher new version number and pins it (keeps agent.core.version monotonically increasing; clients promote to that old content as usual, not rejected by anti-downgrade). For emergency takedown of a bad core.
clientCore.rolledBack: Rolled back (republished old core as higher v{{n}})
clientCore.empty: No core versions yet — click "Upload core version" to start
clientCore.uploadDesc: Upload a single updater-core jar (works on all platforms) and register it as a new version. By default pin to the new version to "update" to this core; clients upgrade on next launch.
clientCore.coreJarHint: One jar works on Windows / macOS / Linux — no per-platform upload needed.
clientCore.pinAfterUpload: Pin to this version right after registering (update to this core)

clientStats.subtitle: Distribution stats (aggregated from pull tracking and telemetry). Machine codes are untrusted, used only as a stat dimension.

clientGuide.intro: The wedge self-locates before the game starts, loads updater-core, fetches the signed manifest, incrementally updates client resources, then launches the game; on network failure or config error it always launches anyway (fail-static / fail-open) and never blocks startup. Follow the steps below to integrate the updater into your modpack and ship it to players.
clientGuide.step1Title: ① Download the two updater jars
clientGuide.step1Desc: Download wedge.jar (the wedge) and updater-core.jar (the update core).
clientGuide.notEmbedded: Some jars are not embedded (build needs `make embed-client-updater`)
clientGuide.step2Desc: Put both jars and jm-updater.json into the jm-updater subfolder of the game directory:
clientGuide.step3Desc: Below is this channel's config. Replace key with a pull key created in the Pull Keys tab (the plaintext is shown only once at creation); change endpoint to a publicly reachable distribution address.
clientGuide.keyPlaceholder: Create a key in the Pull Keys tab, then paste it here
clientGuide.step4Title: ④ Add the JVM argument in the launcher
clientGuide.step4Desc: Add the line below to the custom JVM arguments in HMCL / PCL2 (relative path, recommended). Launchers cd into the .minecraft directory before starting the game, so the relative path resolves against the game directory; omitting =gameDir lets the wedge read the absolute gameDir from the MC command line's --gameDir.
clientGuide.step4Abs: If your launcher does not cd into .minecraft, use an absolute path:
clientGuide.behaviorFailStatic: Offline / endpoint unreachable → launches with the existing local version (fail-static).
clientGuide.behaviorFailOpen: Missing / invalid config → skips the update and launches directly (fail-open). Never blocks startup.
clientGuide.behaviorProgress: A standalone progress window (bar + speed + ETA) appears during updates; degrades to text in headless environments.
clientGuide.behaviorMultiAgent: Coexists with other -javaagent such as authlib-injector (third-party login), order-independent.
clientGuide.downloadFailed: Download failed (jar may not be embedded)

clientDistFlow.title: How does distribution work?
clientDistFlow.subtitle: First release vs. day-to-day updates at a glance
clientDistFlow.first1: Create a distribution channel (one per server).
clientDistFlow.first2: Create a pull key — the player updater's keycard. Keep it safe and viewable anytime; lose it and players stop receiving updates.
clientDistFlow.first3: Publish the first version: upload files or a zip modpack → arrange the directory tree → publish, and the system signs it automatically.
clientDistFlow.first4: Pack it for players: bundle wedge.jar + updater-core.jar + config (channel + key) into the modpack.
clientDistFlow.first5: Hand out the modpack once — you never have to re-send the modpack again.
clientDistFlow.daily1: You publish a new version in the panel.
clientDistFlow.daily2: Players update automatically on launch: wedge → loads updater-core → fetches the signed manifest with the key → verifies the signature → downloads only changed files → enters the game.
clientDistFlow.daily3: If anything goes wrong, it rolls back to the last good version automatically and players still get in.
clientDistFlow.tipPackage: The modpack ships only once; from then on this system handles updates automatically — no need to re-send the modpack.
clientDistFlow.tipKey: Don't lose the key — it's the player's update keycard (its plaintext is viewable anytime here); if it's truly lost, rotate it and re-send the config.
clientDistFlow.tipWedge: The wedge is fixed and rarely touched; only the updater-core is what you upgrade or roll back.

templates.title: Server Templates
templates.subtitle: Reusable templates for creating instances; deleting a template does not affect instances already created from it
templates.marketSubtitle: App market: pick a server and deploy it as an instance in one click; deleting a template does not affect existing instances

market.variablesHint: This template has placeholders; values are substituted into the start command

runtimeAssets.title: Runtime & Assets
runtimeAssets.subtitle: Global view of JDK runtimes and the artifact library — references broken down by node/instance, with usage and hot/cold visualized

runtimeAssets.bindMajor: Resolved to this JDK by Java major version
runtimeAssets.jdkInUse: JDK is in use by instances: {{names}}
runtimeAssets.jdkDeleteDescription: Deletable only when no instance references it; in-use deletion is rejected and shows the occupants.
runtimeAssets.name: Name
runtimeAssets.assetInUse: Asset is referenced by {{count}} place(s) and cannot be deleted
runtimeAssets.assetDeleteDescription: Deletable only when reference count is 0; deletion also removes the physical file.
runtimeAssets.assetDeleteReferenced: This asset is referenced by {{count}} place(s); deletion will be rejected. Release the references first.

storage.title: Platform Storage
storage.subtitle: Read-only browsing, usage stats, archive hot/cold and cache cleanup of the Control Plane data root (FHS layout)
storage.adminOnly: Platform storage is accessible to platform administrators only
storage.loadFailed: Failed to load platform storage overview
storage.clearCacheDescription: Removes all temp files under cache/ ({{count}} item(s) · {{size}}). Only the cache is cleared; other directories are untouched. This action cannot be undone.
storage.name: Name

database.title: Database
database.subtitle: Read-only browse of platform database tables and rows; sensitive columns are masked
database.selectTable: Select a table on the left

systemUpdate.title: System Update
systemUpdate.subtitle: Check and upgrade the Control Plane and node Worker binaries. Upgrades are sha256-verified, hot-swapped, and gracefully restarted; running game servers are unaffected under daemon mode.
systemUpdate.notCheckedYet: Click "Check for updates" to fetch the update source and compare component versions.
systemUpdate.notConfigured: Update source (feed_url) is not configured. Set it under the update section of control-plane.yaml, then check again.
systemUpdate.cpUpgradeConfirmDesc: Downloads the new binary, verifies sha256, replaces it, and gracefully restarts the console. The web UI is briefly unavailable during restart and returns on the new version.
systemUpdate.cpUpgradeStarted: Control Plane upgrade started ({{from}} → {{to}}); restarting shortly
systemUpdate.upgradeAllConfirmDesc: Upgrades every online node one by one (serial). Each node downloads, verifies, hot-swaps, and restarts its Worker; game servers stay up under daemon mode.
systemUpdate.nodeUpgradeConfirmDesc: Tells the node to download the new Worker, verify sha256, replace it, and restart. Running game servers stay up under daemon mode.

logs.title: Log Center
logs.followingHint: Live tailing · newest logs pinned to top

users.title: User Management
users.roleHint: Members can only access authorized resources; group admins manage their group's instances and members; platform admins have full access.
users.roleDesc_0: Member: can only access resources in authorized groups; cannot manage users or nodes.
users.roleDesc_1: Group admin: can manage this group's instances, members and quota.
users.roleDesc_10: Platform admin: full access including users, nodes and platform settings.

alerts.title: Alert Management
alerts.name: Name
alerts.noChannelsHint: No channels yet; create one in the Channels tab first
alerts.inappHint: In-app notifications need no external config; alerts appear in the events list.
alerts.envRefRequired: Credential must reference an env var as ${ENV_VAR}
alerts.envRefHint: Sensitive credentials must reference env vars via ${ENV_VAR}; no plaintext stored.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage appearance and platform preferences
settings.appearanceDesc: Customize the interface theme and language
settings.moreDesc: More platform options will be available later
settings.platformDesc: Server-side platform config; editable items take effect on save (overriding env/YAML defaults), read-only items show the current effective value
settings.editableHint: Overrides the default immediately after saving.
settings.readOnlyHint: To change these, edit the config file / environment variables and restart.
settings.effectTimingHint: This does not take effect immediately in the Control Plane; it requires a config change/restart or applies on the Worker side.
settings.unsavedDesc: Switching categories will discard unsaved changes in the current category. Continue?
settings.securityNotice: These are startup-fixed service/database/auth parameters shown read-only. To change them, edit the config/environment variables and restart.

catDesc.logging: Log level and other runtime logging settings
catDesc.runtime: JDK download mirrors and instance runtime behavior
catDesc.network: Outbound proxy (self-update/JDK/server jar downloads go through it); also the default proxy for all nodes

settings.networkNotice: Global outbound proxy: the Control Plane's own downloads go through it, and it serves as the default proxy for all nodes (each node can override it on the node page). Takes effect immediately, no restart needed. Addresses containing credentials are masked when displayed.

licenses.title: Open-source licenses
licenses.subtitle: This product uses the open-source dependencies below; versions and licenses are scanned at build time.
licenses.empty: No dependency data (generated at build time)

validation.absPath: Must be an absolute path (e.g. /servers/x or C:/x)
validation.envRef: Must be an environment variable reference ${VAR_NAME}
validation.host: Must be a valid hostname or IP
validation.nonNegativeNumber: Must be a non-negative number (0 means unlimited)
